document: Port 20/tcp — ftp-data. IANA description: File Transfer [Default Data]. Role: FTP data-transfer channel; used only in active-mode FTP, opened by the server back to the client after a PORT command on control port 21. Attack surface: Plaintext data exfiltration/ingest, FTP bounce attacks, and abuse of the data channel paired with control-port exploits. Detection hints (service-specific): Data channel opening without a preceding control session on 21; large unidirectional transfers indicating file exfiltration or upload. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2010-4221 (HIGH): Multiple stack-based buffer overflows in the pr_netio_telnet_gets function in netio.c in ProFTPD before 1.3.3c…; CVE-2015-3306 (HIGH): The mod_copy module in ProFTPD 1.3.5 allows remote attackers to read and write to arbitrary files via the site…; CVE-2019-12815 (CRITICAL): An arbitrary file copy vulnerability in mod_copy in ProFTPD up to 1.3.5b allows for remote code execution and…. Normal baseline: Active-mode port 20 is frequently dropped by firewalls/NAT, so legitimate traffic here is uncommon; anomalous activity usually means a legacy device or an attempt to bypass firewall rules. Large transfers on the data channel can indicate file exfiltration or upload.

Port 21/tcp — ftp. IANA description: File Transfer Protocol [Control]. Role: FTP control channel for file transfer; carries credentials and commands in plaintext. Attack surface: Brute-force/anonymous login, plaintext credential capture, and remote exploits in FTP server software (vsftpd/proftpd). Detection hints (service-specific): Many '530' auth-failure responses, anonymous-login attempts, or SITE/mod_copy command abuse. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2010-4221 (HIGH): Multiple stack-based buffer overflows in the pr_netio_telnet_gets function in netio.c in ProFTPD before 1.3.3c…; CVE-2015-3306 (HIGH): The mod_copy module in ProFTPD 1.3.5 allows remote attackers to read and write to arbitrary files via the site…; CVE-2019-12815 (CRITICAL): An arbitrary file copy vulnerability in mod_copy in ProFTPD up to 1.3.5b allows for remote code execution and…. Normal baseline: Legitimate FTP login is from known clients; many short connection attempts to port 21 across hosts indicate brute-force or scanning.

Port 22/tcp — ssh. IANA description: The Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol. Role: SSH remote administration: encrypted shell, file transfer (SCP/SFTP), and tunneling. Attack surface: Credential brute-force, password spraying, key/credential reuse, and remote vulnerabilities in SSH implementations (OpenSSH, Dropbear on IoT/network gear). Detection hints (service-specific): Pre-auth protocol-version banner grabbing, repeated key-exchange without completing authentication, or successful auth following many failures. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2010-4478 (CRITICAL): OpenSSH 5.6 and earlier, when J-PAKE is enabled, does not properly validate the public parameters in the J-PAK…; CVE-2016-7406 (CRITICAL): Format string vulnerability in Dropbear SSH before 2016.74 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code v…; CVE-2016-7407 (CRITICAL): The dropbearconvert command in Dropbear SSH before 2016.74 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via a cr…. Normal baseline: Legitimate SSH is interactive and from known admin hosts; repeated short failed connections from one source across many destinations indicate brute-force or scanning.

Port 23/tcp — telnet. IANA description: Telnet. Role: Telnet remote terminal: unencrypted command-line access (legacy). Attack surface: Plaintext credential sniffing, default/weak-credential login, and automated mass-exploitation by IoT botnets (Mirai-class) that spread via exposed Telnet. Detection hints (service-specific): Plaintext credentials visible in the stream, default-credential login prompts, and connections to embedded/IoT devices. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2016-7115 (CRITICAL): Buffer overflow in the handle_packet function in mactelnet.c in the client in MAC-Telnet 0.4.3 and earlier all…; CVE-2020-10188 (CRITICAL): utility.c in telnetd in netkit telnet through 0.17 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via short…; CVE-2022-39028 (HIGH): telnetd in GNU Inetutils through 2.3, MIT krb5-appl through 1.0.3, and derivative works has a NULL pointer der…. Normal baseline: Telnet is obsolete and rare in modern networks; a single SYN to port 23 across many destination hosts is characteristic of horizontal scanning, not legitimate use.

Port 25/tcp — smtp. IANA description: Simple Mail Transfer. Role: SMTP mail transfer between mail servers and from clients. Attack surface: Open-relay abuse, spam injection, user enumeration (VRFY/EXPN), credential brute-force, and remote exploits in MTAs (Exim/Postfix/Sendmail). Detection hints (service-specific): VRFY/EXPN user enumeration, open-relay tests (external sender + external recipient), and malformed HELO/EHLO. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2010-4344 (CRITICAL): Heap-based buffer overflow in the string_vformat function in string.c in Exim before 4.70 allows remote attack…; CVE-2017-16943 (CRITICAL): The receive_msg function in receive.c in the SMTP daemon in Exim 4.88 and 4.89 allows remote attackers to exec…; CVE-2018-6789 (CRITICAL): An issue was discovered in the base64d function in the SMTP listener in Exim before 4.90.1. By sending a handc…. Normal baseline: Legitimate SMTP is server-to-server or authenticated submission; probing many hosts on 25 suggests relay-hunting or scanning.

Port 53/udp — domain (DNS). IANA description: Domain Name Server. Role: DNS name resolution; UDP for most queries, TCP for large responses, DNSSEC, zone transfers, and increasingly DNS-over-TLS. Attack surface: Cache poisoning, amplification/reflection DDoS, zone-transfer (AXFR) leakage, DNS tunneling for C2/exfiltration, and resolver software vulnerabilities (BIND/dnsmasq). Detection hints (service-specific): High-entropy or excessively long subdomains (tunneling), oversized/abnormal TXT records (C2 beaconing), AXFR zone-transfer requests, and query-volume spikes (amplification). Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2017-14492 (CRITICAL): Heap-based buffer overflow in dnsmasq before 2.78 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash)…; CVE-2017-14493 (CRITICAL): Stack-based buffer overflow in dnsmasq before 2.78 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash…; CVE-2017-14491 (CRITICAL): Heap-based buffer overflow in dnsmasq before 2.78 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash)…. Normal baseline: Normal DNS is short query/response to a resolver (UDP, or TCP for DNSSEC/DoT/large answers — TCP/53 is no longer only AXFR). Anomalies include fragmented packets, abuse of TXT records (C2 beaconing), high-entropy subdomains (tunneling), and large query volumes (amplification).

Port 80/tcp — http. IANA description: World Wide Web HTTP. Role: HTTP web service / web application and device management UIs. Attack surface: Web-app exploitation (injection, RCE, auth bypass), exploitation of embedded web servers on IoT/routers (lighttpd/boa/GoAhead), and mass scanning for vulnerable endpoints. Detection hints (service-specific): Scanner user-agents, path-traversal or injection patterns in URIs, requests to admin/management endpoints, and web-shell upload attempts. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2010-0425 (HIGH): modules/arch/win32/mod_isapi.c in mod_isapi in the Apache HTTP Server 2.0.37 through 2.0.63, 2.2.0 through 2.2…; CVE-2014-2323 (CRITICAL): SQL injection vulnerability in mod_mysql_vhost.c in lighttpd before 1.4.35 allows remote attackers to execute…; CVE-2016-0746 (CRITICAL): Use-after-free vulnerability in the resolver in nginx 0.6.18 through 1.8.0 and 1.9.x before 1.9.10 allows remo…. Normal baseline: Legitimate HTTP is varied client traffic to known sites; rapid sequential requests across many hosts or to odd URIs indicate scanning or exploitation attempts.

Port 110/tcp — pop3. IANA description: Post Office Protocol - Version 3. Role: POP3 mailbox retrieval (download-and-delete email access). Attack surface: Plaintext credential capture, brute-force login, and mail-server software vulnerabilities (Dovecot). Detection hints (service-specific): Repeated USER/PASS auth failures and plaintext credentials in the stream. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2019-11500 (CRITICAL): In Dovecot before 2.2.36.4 and 2.3.x before 2.3.7.2 (and Pigeonhole before 0.5.7.2), protocol processing can f…; CVE-2019-7524 (HIGH): In Dovecot before 2.2.36.3 and 2.3.x before 2.3.5.1, a local attacker can cause a buffer overflow in the index…; CVE-2022-30550 (HIGH): An issue was discovered in the auth component in Dovecot 2.2 and 2.3 before 2.3.20. When two passdb configurat…. Normal baseline: Legitimate POP3 is from known mail clients; many login attempts across hosts indicate brute-force or scanning.

Port 111/tcp — sunrpc. IANA description: SUN Remote Procedure Call. Role: ONC/SunRPC portmapper (rpcbind): maps RPC program numbers to ports. Attack surface: RPC service enumeration, amplification DDoS via rpcbind, and exploitation of exposed RPC services (NFS, etc.). Detection hints (service-specific): rpcinfo/portmapper dumps enumerating registered RPC programs, often preceding access to NFS or other RPC services. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2009-2753 (HIGH): Multiple buffer overflows in the authentication functionality in librpc.dll in the Informix Storage Manager (I…; CVE-2009-2754 (HIGH): Integer signedness error in the authentication functionality in librpc.dll in the Informix Storage Manager (IS…; CVE-2010-4070 (HIGH): Integer overflow in librpc.dll in portmap.exe (aka the ISM Portmapper service) in ISM before 2.20.TC1.117 in I…. Normal baseline: rpcbind should not be Internet-exposed; external probes to 111 are reconnaissance for RPC services or amplification abuse.

Port 135/tcp — epmap. IANA description: DCE endpoint resolution. Role: Microsoft RPC endpoint mapper (DCE/RPC / DCOM): locates Windows RPC services. Attack surface: RPC/DCOM remote exploits, lateral movement, and service enumeration; historically high-impact Windows worms. Detection hints (service-specific): Endpoint-mapper queries enumerating RPC service bindings, typically followed by a connection to a high dynamic RPC port. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2019-10918 (HIGH): A vulnerability has been identified in SIMATIC PCS 7 V8.0 and earlier (All versions), SIMATIC PCS 7 V8.1 (All…; CVE-2021-3738 (HIGH): In DCE/RPC it is possible to share the handles (cookies for resource state) between multiple connections via a…; CVE-2013-4688 (HIGH): flowd in Juniper Junos 10.4 before 10.4R11 on SRX devices, when the MSRPC Application Layer Gateway (ALG) is e…. Normal baseline: 135 is internal Windows infrastructure traffic; external connections or scanning of 135 are reconnaissance or exploitation attempts and should not cross the perimeter.

Port 139/tcp — netbios-ssn. IANA description: NETBIOS Session Service. Role: NetBIOS Session Service: legacy SMB-over-NetBIOS file sharing on Windows (largely superseded by direct SMB on 445). Attack surface: SMB/NetBIOS enumeration, null-session information disclosure, credential relay, and legacy SMB exploits (Samba/Windows). Detection hints (service-specific): NetBIOS name queries, null-session establishment, and legacy SMB negotiation for information disclosure. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2012-1182 (HIGH): The RPC code generator in Samba 3.x before 3.4.16, 3.5.x before 3.5.14, and 3.6.x before 3.6.4 does not implem…; CVE-2015-0240 (HIGH): The Netlogon server implementation in smbd in Samba 3.5.x and 3.6.x before 3.6.25, 4.0.x before 4.0.25, 4.1.x…; CVE-2017-7494 (CRITICAL): Samba since version 3.5.0 and before 4.6.4, 4.5.10 and 4.4.14 is vulnerable to remote code execution vulnerabi…. Normal baseline: Modern SMB uses 445 directly, so 139 traffic today is mostly legacy systems or fallback/scanning; external exposure or probing indicates reconnaissance or attempted lateral movement.

Port 143/tcp — imap. IANA description: Internet Message Access Protocol. Role: IMAP mailbox access (server-side mail management). Attack surface: Plaintext credential capture, brute-force login, and IMAP server vulnerabilities (Dovecot). Detection hints (service-specific): IMAP capability probing and repeated LOGIN auth failures with plaintext credentials. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2019-11500 (CRITICAL): In Dovecot before 2.2.36.4 and 2.3.x before 2.3.7.2 (and Pigeonhole before 0.5.7.2), protocol processing can f…; CVE-2019-7524 (HIGH): In Dovecot before 2.2.36.3 and 2.3.x before 2.3.5.1, a local attacker can cause a buffer overflow in the index…; CVE-2022-30550 (HIGH): An issue was discovered in the auth component in Dovecot 2.2 and 2.3 before 2.3.20. When two passdb configurat…. Normal baseline: Legitimate IMAP is from known clients; many login attempts across hosts indicate brute-force or scanning.

Port 443/tcp — https. IANA description: http protocol over TLS/SSL. Role: HTTPS: HTTP over TLS for web apps and device management UIs. Attack surface: TLS-stack vulnerabilities (OpenSSL — e.g. Heartbleed-class), web-app exploitation under TLS, and exploitation of embedded HTTPS on routers/IoT. Detection hints (service-specific): TLS-metadata anomalies: JA3/JA3S fingerprint mismatch, unusual or absent SNI, self-signed certificates, and periodic same-size handshakes consistent with C2 beaconing. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2009-3245 (HIGH): OpenSSL before 0.9.8m does not check for a NULL return value from bn_wexpand function calls in (1) crypto/bn/b…; CVE-2010-0425 (HIGH): modules/arch/win32/mod_isapi.c in mod_isapi in the Apache HTTP Server 2.0.37 through 2.0.63, 2.2.0 through 2.2…; CVE-2014-2323 (CRITICAL): SQL injection vulnerability in mod_mysql_vhost.c in lighttpd before 1.4.35 allows remote attackers to execute…. Normal baseline: Legitimate HTTPS is normal client traffic to known services; anomalous handshakes, scanning across many hosts, or odd byte patterns warrant scrutiny.

Port 445/tcp — microsoft-ds (SMB). IANA description: Microsoft-DS. Role: Microsoft-DS / direct-hosted SMB: Windows file sharing and named pipes. Attack surface: SMBv1 remote exploits (EternalBlue/MS17-010-class) and worm/ransomware propagation; on SMBv2/v3, NTLM relay and ransomware lateral movement; Samba RCE on Linux. Detection hints (service-specific): Access to administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$), SMBv1 dialect negotiation (EternalBlue indicator), named-pipe RPC use, and NTLM relay patterns. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2012-1182 (HIGH): The RPC code generator in Samba 3.x before 3.4.16, 3.5.x before 3.5.14, and 3.6.x before 3.6.4 does not implem…; CVE-2015-0240 (HIGH): The Netlogon server implementation in smbd in Samba 3.5.x and 3.6.x before 3.6.25, 4.0.x before 4.0.25, 4.1.x…; CVE-2017-7494 (CRITICAL): Samba since version 3.5.0 and before 4.6.4, 4.5.10 and 4.4.14 is vulnerable to remote code execution vulnerabi…. Normal baseline: 445 is internal Windows file sharing; risk splits by version — SMBv1 negotiation is high-risk (EternalBlue/worms), while SMBv2/v3 abuse centers on NTLM relay and lateral movement. External exposure or scanning is a common ransomware/worm entry vector.

Port 992/tcp — telnets. IANA description: telnet protocol over TLS/SSL. Role: Telnet over TLS (telnets): encrypted variant of legacy Telnet. Attack surface: Same as Telnet (credential/login attacks) but TLS-wrapped; rarely deployed, so exposure is itself suspicious. Detection hints (service-specific): TLS-wrapped Telnet negotiation; because telnets is rarely deployed, any handshake here is itself a notable indicator. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2020-10188 (CRITICAL): utility.c in telnetd in netkit telnet through 0.17 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via short…; CVE-2022-39028 (HIGH): telnetd in GNU Inetutils through 2.3, MIT krb5-appl through 1.0.3, and derivative works has a NULL pointer der…; CVE-2010-4221 (HIGH): Multiple stack-based buffer overflows in the pr_netio_telnet_gets function in netio.c in ProFTPD before 1.3.3c…. Normal baseline: telnets is very rarely used; connections or scanning of 992 are unusual and most often reconnaissance.

Port 4848/tcp — appserv-http (GlassFish admin). IANA description: App Server - Admin HTTP. Role: GlassFish Java EE application-server admin console (management interface over HTTP/HTTPS). Attack surface: Credential brute-force on the admin login, authentication-bypass exploits, and malicious application (WAR) deployment leading to remote code execution. Detection hints (service-specific): POST requests to the admin login endpoint, access to the GlassFish REST admin API, and application (WAR) deployment calls. Representative known vulnerabilities (NVD, top by CVSS): CVE-2011-0807 (HIGH): Unspecified vulnerability in Oracle Sun GlassFish Enterprise Server 2.1, 2.1.1, and 3.0.1, and Sun Java System…; CVE-2015-7182 (CRITICAL): Heap-based buffer overflow in the ASN.1 decoder in Mozilla Network Security Services (NSS) before 3.19.2.1 and…; CVE-2016-3607 (CRITICAL): Unspecified vulnerability in the Oracle GlassFish Server component in Oracle Fusion Middleware 3.0.1 and 3.1.2…. Normal baseline: Legitimate admin access is rare, from internal IPs, during business hours, and interactive; external access, off-hours, or many short connections are anomalous.
